hypotheses.title: Forward-influence routing improves the committed FOCUS-Fabric baseline at matched active bytes
hypotheses.rationale: Test whether a forward-influence routing change reduces in-distribution output NMSE without changing active bytes or weakening the declared token-safety checks.
controls: committed FOCUS-Fabric baseline under the identical benchmark configuration
hypotheses.disconfirming_condition: No >=5% objective improvement at matched active bytes, or any token-agreement regression.

hypotheses.title: Offline consolidation into fast weights can reduce active archive traffic without policy-memory loss
hypotheses.rationale: Separate online exact recoverability from scheduled consolidation; immutable semantic records must remain externally auditable.
hypotheses.disconfirming_condition: Any governance-memory erasure, provenance break, or exact sequence mismatch.

hypotheses.title: Sparse exact sentinel audits can detect exchangeability failure before routing safety collapses
hypotheses.rationale: Split-conformal coverage is marginal and can fail under shift. Online audit residuals should trigger page recompilation or stricter fallback.
hypotheses.disconfirming_condition: No reduction in false accepts at equal or lower exact-audit traffic.

hypotheses.title: Layer/head/page rate allocation beats independent local budgets under a global HBM constraint
hypotheses.rationale: Sensitivity and semantic retrieval specialization vary by layer and head; local ratios waste budget.
hypotheses.disconfirming_condition: No improvement on held-out traces, or allocation becomes unstable across seeds.
